records.ety: [OF. avochier, LL. advocare to recognize the existence of a thing, to advocate, fr. L. advocare to call to; ad + vocare to call. Cf. Avow to declare, Advocate, and see Vouch, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Avouched (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Avouching.]

defs: To appeal to; to cite or claim as authority.
quotes: They avouch many successions of authorities.

defs: To maintain a just or true; to vouch for.
quotes: We might be disposed to question its authenticity, it if were not avouched by the full evidence.

defs: To declare or assert positively and as matter of fact; to affirm openly.
quotes: If this which he avouches does appear.
Such antiquities could have been avouched for the Irish.

defs: To acknowledge deliberately; to admit; to confess; to sanction.
quotes: Thou hast avouched the Lord this day to be thy God.
The sensible and true avouch Of mine own eyes.
